vessels.name: Yamato
vessels.armament: 9× 460mm guns (largest naval guns ever fitted to a warship), 12× 155mm guns, 24× 127mm AA guns
vessels.fate: Sunk 7 April 1945 by US aircraft (386 aircraft) during Operation Ten-Go
vessels.notes: The largest battleship ever built. Designed to outfight any Allied battleship in a decisive engagement that never came. Sunk carrying only enough fuel for a one-way mission to Okinawa.

vessels.name: Shōkaku
vessels.fate: Sunk 19 June 1944 by USS Cavalla (submarine) at Battle of the Philippine Sea
vessels.notes: One of Japan's finest carriers. Participated in the attack on Pearl Harbor, the Battle of the Coral Sea, and the Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands before being sunk by torpedo.

vessels.name: I-400 class submarine
vessels.notes: The largest submarines built before nuclear-powered boats. Designed to launch aircraft for strikes on the US mainland (Panama Canal, US East Coast cities). Surrendered before their missions could be executed.

vessels.name: Marasti
vessels.notes: Romania's most capable warship. Operated in the Black Sea against Soviet forces, conducting minelaying operations and shore bombardment in support of Axis advances in 1941–1942.
